text:
Once upon a time in a small forest, there was a wise old owl named Oliver. Every morning, Oliver would glide gracefully through the trees, listening to the forest's quiet songs.

One day, as he was swooping down to catch breakfast, he noticed a group of animals sitting on a branch. They were discussing something intensely—the little owl wasn't sure what.

"Could it be?" the first animal whispered.

"No," the second critter replied. "Our leader, Owlelda, insists they're planning a big festival. They're building something... a birdhouse!"

Oliver tilted his head. Birdhouses? That sounded unusual. He squawked his curiosity.

"What do they call it?" Oliver asked.

"A Feathers' Gathering!"

Oliver hooted happily. "Sounds delightful!"

As the animals laughed and chatted about their crafty project, Oliver couldn't resist adding his own commentary. "Oh yes! Perhaps your feathers will make the best ornaments. We could start
